sections.paragraph:
θρηνέω impf. ἐθρήνουν; fut. θρηνήσω; 1 aor. ἐθρήνησα; fut. pass. 3 sg. θρηνηθήσεται Mi 2:4 (s. next entry; Hom. et al.; LXX; TestSol 1:6 D; TestAbr B 6 p. 110, 1 [Stone p. 68]; TestZeb 4:5; ApcEsdr 5:27 p. 31, 1 Tdf.; Ar.; Just.) gener. ‘to express oneself in grief’, freq. in sounds and rhythms established by funereal custom in various regions of the ancient world (cp. Il. 24, 722; Od. 24, 61).

① to express oneself in sorrowful tones, mourn, lament intr. (w. κλαίειν Just., D. 141, 3; cp. Jo 1:5) J 16:20.

② to express oneself in a song or hymn of grief, sing a dirge intr. (Aesop., Fab. 369 H. [female mourners]; LXX) Mt 11:17; Lk 7:32. As v.l. ἐθρήνησε for ἐποίησεν θρῆνον GJs 3:1 (cod. A).

③ to mourn for someone in ritual fashion, mourn for, lament τινά someone (Herodian 3, 4, 6; Nicetas Eugen. 7, 182 H.; LXX; Philo, Leg. All. 3, 231; Jos., Bell. 3, 436; Ar. 8, 2; Just., D. 95, 2 ἑαυτούς) Lk 23:27 (w. κόπτεσθαι as Xenophanes: Vorsokr. 21 Testimon. A 13; Mi 1:8; Jos., Ant. 6, 377; s. EMartino, Morte e pianto rituale nel mondo antico, ’58, 195–235). δύο θρήνους ἐθρήνει GJs 2:1. S. κόπτω 2.—PHeinisch, Die Totenklage im AT, ’31; CWestermann, Struktur u. Geschichte der Klage im AT: ZAW 66, ’54, 44–80; PFerris, The Genre of Communal Lament in the Bible and the Ancient Near East, ’92 (OT); CBuresch, Consolationum a Graecis Romanisque scriptarum historia critica 1886; EReiner, Die Rituelle Totenklage der Griechen ’38; MAlexiou, The Ritual Lament in Greek Tradition ’74. Add. reff. Betz, SM 120.—DELG s.v. θρῆνος. On the semantic field s. Schmidt, Syn. III 378ff. M-M. TW.

impf. impf. = imperfect

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. sg. = singular

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

TestZeb TestZeb = Testament of Zebulon, s. Test12Patr—List 2

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

gener. gener. = generally

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Il. Il. = Iliad, s. Hom.—List 5

Od Od , s. Hom.; VIII? b.c.—List 5

intr. intr. = intransitive

Aesop Aesop = a calque for a variety of lit. associated with the name of Aesop—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

Nicetas Eugen Nicetas Eugen , XII a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Xenophanes Xenophanes , VI–V b.c.—List 5

Vorsokr. Vorsokr. = Vorsokratiker, their fragments, ed. by HDiels/WKranz (the term Pre-Socratic[s] is used when no specific ref. is made to the German ed.)—Lists 5, 6

ZAW ZAW = Zeitschrift für die Alttestamentliche Wissenschaft—Lists 6

OT OT = Old Testament

Betz, SM Betz, SM = The Sermon on the Mount (Hermeneia)—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Schmidt, Schmidt, Syn. = JS., Synonymik—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 458
